title: GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: GoDaddy leverages its massive position as the world's fifth-largest web host and dominant domain registrar to drive significant, highly recurring free cash flow ($1.3B annually). While top-line growth is a solid 6.8%, the real value proposition lies in its ability to cross-sell higher-margin services—like website builders, commerce tools, and email—to its vast base of micro and small business customers. The low capital intensity of this model is highly attractive.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: GoDaddy maintains solid competitive momentum. Its core domain registration business serves as a massive funnel to cross-sell increasingly comprehensive software and commerce solutions.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: At 6.8% revenue growth, GoDaddy outperforms many legacy tech peers, demonstrating success in expanding ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) despite a maturing domain registration market. Its recurring revenue streams continue to provide a solid foundation for incremental top-line expansion.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: While it faces intense competition from platform builders like Shopify or Wix on the commerce side, GoDaddy maintains a dominant, top-tier market share in initial domain registration and basic web hosting. The company remains the default starting point for many new micro-businesses.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power is robust for domain renewals and essential hosting, though less pronounced in the highly competitive website builder and e-commerce software markets. It frequently relies on promotional introductory pricing followed by higher renewal rates.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Product development is steady, focused heavily on integrating AI tools (like GoDaddy Airo) to simplify website creation and commerce for micro-businesses. Though it rarely defines new software categories, its execution on incremental updates is reliable.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The company's economic moat is incredibly durable, characterized by exceptionally high switching costs once a small business establishes its digital identity and operations on GoDaddy's platform.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For a small business, migrating a domain, website, integrated email, and potentially point-of-sale systems to a new provider is fraught with operational risk and downtime, creating massive lock-in. Customers are often hesitant to risk disrupting their online presence.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: GoDaddy does not benefit significantly from direct network effects. The value of its hosting or domain services does not scale exponentially based on the total number of users.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company operates within the relatively stable regulatory framework of ICANN for domain registration. It does, however, face standard data privacy and cybersecurity compliance challenges common to all global hosting providers.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: By operating a massively scaled, largely self-serve software platform, GoDaddy converts an exceptional amount of its revenue into free cash flow ($1.3B) with relatively low ongoing capital requirements. Its scale provides a massive advantage over smaller registrars.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment is generally positive, buoyed by the company's consistent cash generation, successful margin expansion initiatives, and ongoing aggressive share repurchases.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates have been supportive, reflecting successful cost optimization efforts and the steady, high-margin growth of its 'Applications & Commerce' segment. Analysts are generally favorable on its free cash flow trajectory.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative has largely shifted from viewing GoDaddy simply as a cheap domain registrar to recognizing it as a comprehensive, cash-generating SaaS platform for micro-businesses. It is increasingly viewed as a value-oriented tech stock.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management executes exceptionally well on capital allocation, directing the substantial $1.3B in annual free cash flow primarily toward aggressive, value-accretive share repurchases. They have avoided value-destroying, overly ambitious acquisitions.

keyRisks: Intense competition from sophisticated, specialized platform builders (e.g., Shopify for e-commerce, Wix/Squarespace for design) capturing the high-value software segment.
Macroeconomic weakness disproportionately impacting the formation and survival rates of the micro and small businesses that make up GoDaddy's core customer base.
Cybersecurity breaches or extended platform outages severely damaging brand trust and driving catastrophic churn.
keyCatalysts: Commerce attach rate expansion: only ~5% of GoDaddy's 21M+ customers use commerce tools — increasing penetration to 10% would nearly double Applications & Commerce revenue
GoDaddy Payments scaling toward $50B+ annualized volume provides a high-margin revenue stream that monetizes the existing customer base without additional acquisition cost
AI-driven ARPU expansion: Airo and other AI tools enable GoDaddy to offer higher-value services (marketing automation, SEO optimization) that increase revenue per customer
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored GDDY at 72/100 and Opus at 71/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
